recommendations: [MNT003] Create GitHub releases for meaningful milestones.
[MNT002] Use GitHub issues to track bugs, questions, and completed maintenance work.
[MNT004] Make contribution paths clear and acknowledge contributors when they appear.

rule_results.title: README exists
rule_results.recommendation: Add a README that explains what the project does and how to use it.

rule_results.title: README has meaningful content
rule_results.recommendation: Expand the README beyond a title with practical setup and usage details.

rule_results.title: Project description is present
rule_results.explanation: Repository metadata contains a useful description.
rule_results.recommendation: Add a concise value proposition in the README or repository metadata.

rule_results.title: Installation instructions are present
rule_results.recommendation: Document installation steps such as pip, uv, Poetry, or source installs.

rule_results.title: Usage instructions are present
rule_results.recommendation: Show at least one command or code example for normal project usage.

rule_results.title: License file exists
rule_results.recommendation: Add an OSI-approved license file so users know the reuse terms.

rule_results.title: Changelog exists
rule_results.recommendation: Add a CHANGELOG to make project evolution easy to scan.

rule_results.title: Contributing guide exists
rule_results.recommendation: Add CONTRIBUTING.md with setup, testing, and contribution expectations.

rule_results.title: Screenshots or demos are referenced
rule_results.explanation: README references screenshots, demos, or sample output.
rule_results.recommendation: Add a screenshot, terminal capture, GIF, demo link, or example output.

rule_results.title: Tests directory exists
rule_results.recommendation: Add a tests/ directory with meaningful automated tests.

rule_results.title: Test files exist
rule_results.recommendation: Add pytest or unittest files that cover behavior, not only imports.

rule_results.title: GitHub Actions workflow exists
rule_results.recommendation: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on push and pull_request.

rule_results.title: Workflow runs tests
rule_results.explanation: A CI workflow appears to run tests.
rule_results.recommendation: Run pytest, unittest, tox, or nox from CI.

rule_results.title: Coverage configuration exists
rule_results.explanation: Coverage configuration or CI coverage command is present.
rule_results.recommendation: Configure coverage.py or pytest-cov and enforce a threshold.

rule_results.title: Lint configuration exists
rule_results.explanation: Lint configuration or CI lint command is present.
rule_results.recommendation: Configure a linter such as Ruff and run it in CI.

rule_results.title: Type-check configuration exists
rule_results.explanation: Type-check configuration or CI command is present.
rule_results.recommendation: Configure mypy or pyright and run type checks in CI.

rule_results.title: Source directory structure exists
rule_results.explanation: A src/ package layout is present.
rule_results.recommendation: Use a clear package layout such as src/package_name/.

rule_results.title: Dependency manifest exists
rule_results.recommendation: Add pyproject.toml, requirements.txt, setup.cfg, or an equivalent manifest.

rule_results.title: .gitignore exists
rule_results.recommendation: Add .gitignore entries for caches, virtualenvs, secrets, and build outputs.

rule_results.title: Example environment file exists
rule_results.explanation: An example environment file is present.
rule_results.recommendation: Add .env.example or .env.sample documenting optional environment variables.

rule_results.title: Modular Python organization exists
rule_results.explanation: Found 17 Python source files outside tests.
rule_results.recommendation: Split non-trivial behavior into focused modules instead of one large script.

rule_results.title: Package metadata exists
rule_results.recommendation: Populate [project] metadata such as name, version, description, and Python support.

rule_results.title: Dependencies are declared
rule_results.recommendation: Declare runtime dependencies in pyproject.toml or requirements files.

rule_results.title: Command-line entry point exists
rule_results.explanation: A command-line script entry point is declared.
rule_results.recommendation: Expose CLI tools through [project.scripts] or equivalent entry points.

rule_results.title: No obvious committed secrets
rule_results.explanation: No high-confidence committed secret patterns were detected.
rule_results.recommendation: Remove committed secrets, rotate exposed credentials, and keep real values out of Git.

rule_results.title: .env is ignored
rule_results.recommendation: Add .env to .gitignore while keeping .env.example tracked.

rule_results.title: Dependency versions are constrained
rule_results.explanation: Dependency declarations include version constraints or lock files.
rule_results.recommendation: Use version ranges or lock files so dependency changes are intentional.

rule_results.title: Exception handling is present
rule_results.explanation: Python source contains explicit exception handling.
rule_results.recommendation: Handle expected network, parsing, and filesystem errors explicitly.

rule_results.title: Input validation is present
rule_results.recommendation: Validate URLs, paths, and external data before using them.

rule_results.title: No unsafe eval usage
rule_results.explanation: No unsafe dynamic execution or deserialization was detected.
rule_results.recommendation: Avoid eval, exec, and unsafe deserialization on untrusted input.

rule_results.title: No hardcoded API token patterns
rule_results.explanation: No hardcoded API token patterns were detected.
rule_results.recommendation: Use environment variables or secret stores for tokens and API keys.

rule_results.title: Security policy exists
rule_results.recommendation: Add SECURITY.md with vulnerability reporting guidance.

rule_results.title: Recent commit activity
rule_results.explanation: Default branch was updated 0 day(s) ago.
rule_results.recommendation: Keep the default branch active or explain maintenance status in the README.

rule_results.title: Issue activity is visible
rule_results.recommendation: Use GitHub issues to track bugs, questions, and completed maintenance work.

rule_results.title: Release history exists
rule_results.recommendation: Create GitHub releases for meaningful milestones.

rule_results.title: Contributor activity is visible
rule_results.recommendation: Make contribution paths clear and acknowledge contributors when they appear.

rule_results.title: Repository is not archived
rule_results.recommendation: Unarchive active projects or document that the project is intentionally archived.

rule_results.title: Repository description exists
rule_results.recommendation: Add a concise GitHub repository description.

rule_results.title: Repository topics exist
rule_results.recommendation: Add relevant GitHub topics so the project is easier to discover.

rule_results.title: Default branch is known
rule_results.recommendation: Ensure the repository exposes a clear default branch.

disclaimer: Repo Health Inspector performs lightweight static analysis. It can highlight common readiness signals, but it does not prove that a repository is bug-free, secure, maintained, or production-ready.
report_notes: This report represents the repository state at the recorded analysis timestamp; scores can change when the repository changes.
The analysis is based on lightweight static checks and GitHub metadata when available.
Skipped rules earn 0 points in the raw score; the evaluated score is calculated from non-skipped rules only.
